The following sentences are synthetic placeholder prose for automated tests only.
They are not quotations from any historical philosopher; they exist so the Voice
ingestion stub can split on sentence boundaries and emit anchored claims.

A serious inquiry into the limits of language must acknowledge that what can be said
at all can be said clearly, and whereof one cannot speak thereof one must be silent.
The point of such a discipline is not mysticism but clarity about what counts as a
well-formed claim within a given practice of justification.
